title: Board Game Rules
entries: {pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} moves one of his or her pieces on the board. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} places a piece on the board. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} moves two or more of his or her pieces on the board. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} places two or more pieces on the board. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} removes one or more of his or her opponent's pieces by encircling it or flanking it. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} halts his or her opponent's progress by encircling or flanking one or more of the opponent's pieces. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} converts one or more of his or her opponent's pieces by encircling it or flanking it. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} moves his or her piece closer to a destination space on the board. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-board-winner} The {pick:most powerful piece|strongest maneuver} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-board-worst}.
